title: APC InRow and Airflow Unit Compressor or Fan Fault Alarms
dataSources: `index=power` `sourcetype=snmp:trap` APC enterprise `.1.3.6.1.4.1.318` environmental alarms, or polled cooling status OIDs normalized to `sourcetype=snmp:apc:cooling`
app: Splunk Connect for SNMP (Splunkbase #6266) trap receiver, SNMP Modular Input (Splunkbase #1537)
spl:
index=power (sourcetype=snmp:trap OR sourcetype=snmp:apc:cooling) host=* trapoid="*318*"
| search cooling OR compressor OR Compressor OR fan OR Fan OR InRow OR Airflow OR airflow OR environmental OR Environmental
| table _time, host, trapoid, trap_name, eventName, _raw
description: APC InRow and aisle containment products emit SNMP traps when compressors stall, fans fail, or airflow is inadequate. Landing those traps in Splunk gives the same early warning as UPS events for thermal runaway in edge and small data halls.
value: Prevents thermal shutdowns of IT loads by correlating cooling faults with rack inlet temperatures before CRAC capacity is exhausted.
implementation: Point NMC/trap agents to SC4SNMP trap listener or SNMP MI. Normalize `varbind` values into `alarm_text`. For polled architectures, create `snmp:apc:cooling` with extracted `coolingUnitState` / alarm fields per your MIB walk. Filter vendor maintenance windows. Tie alerts to on-call facilities, not only IT.
visualization: Timeline (cooling traps), Table (host, alarm_text), Sankey (trap → rack group via lookup).
detailedImplementation:
Prerequisites
• Trap sourcetype with `full_json` or `VARBINDS` extracted.
• HVAC vendor contract for onsite response.

Step 1 — Lab inject trap sample.

Step 2 — Deduplicate flapping traps with `streamstats`.

Step 3 — Map to BMS points if dual-monitored.

Step 4 — Thermal runbook links to rack shutdown SOP.
grandmaExplanation: We listen for alarms from in-row air conditioners—like fan or compressor trouble—so the room doesn’t overheat and knock out computers.
